codeReview.summary: The error-classifier split itself looks behavior-preserving: the extracted signal/auth/infra helpers keep the original classification order in `classifyApiError`, and the current agent-loop callers still consume the same categories. However the feature commit cannot pass review because it bundled unrelated release-metadata version bumps into a non-release refactor commit.

issues.description: Commit `a1c32b9bd31f6c722e58a02aa6bc96430e369731` bumps the root package version from `0.4.62` to `0.4.63`, which is outside the scope of this non-release error-handling refactor and violates the mission guardrail against bundling version bumps with refactoring work.

The same feature commit also bumps the Chrome extension manifest version from `0.4.62` to `0.4.63`, widening scope beyond the error-handling refactor and changing release metadata without feature justification.

The Firefox manifest version is bumped in the same refactor commit even though the feature only targets `packages/extension/ai/error-*`; this release-metadata change should have been excluded or isolated.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` checklist is not sufficient by itself to catch hook-added version bumps. In this session the worker staged only `packages/extension/ai/`, but the final commit still contained `package.json` plus both manifest files. Scrutiny/orchestrator should keep treating version-file diffs as a hard post-commit check for non-release refactors rather than relying on staged-diff review or handoff claims alone.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/sero/projects/browser-ai/.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:116-125` tells workers to verify there are no version bumps. The transcript skeleton for worker session `d80f4036-6de9-4c06-b33b-670372c81405` shows `git add packages/extension/ai/` before commit, while `git show --stat a1c32b9bd31f6c722e58a02aa6bc96430e369731` still changes `package.json`, `packages/extension/manifest.json`, and `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission guidance already forbids bundled version bumps, but the worker handoff summary did not mention the three versioned metadata files that were committed. For scope review, handoffs are not sufficient as a source of truth unless they are checked against the actual commit diff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/AGENTS.md:212-216` says non-release refactor features must not bundle package or manifest version bumps. The handoff file `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T10-18-39-942Z__extension-refactor-error-handling__d80f4036-6de9-4c06-b33b-670372c81405.json` summarizes only the four error-handling modules, while `git show --stat a1c32b9bd31f6c722e58a02aa6bc96430e369731` includes the three version files as additional changes.

summary: Reviewed the feature entry, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill instructions, and commit `a1c32b9bd31f6c722e58a02aa6bc96430e369731`. The error-handling refactor itself looks structurally sound, but the review fails because the commit bundled three out-of-scope version bumps into package/manifest metadata.
